key_points: On-campus housing costs $3,000+ per semester, students call it overpriced
Primarily a commuter school — most students live off campus
Off-campus apartments run ~$850/month, seen as better value by grad students
key_quotes: All for the low price of $3000+ a semester... Lower your prices and maybe you wouldn't have to beg students to live on campus.
Just for a change take a look at your ridiculous prices!! Then I can assure you it won't sound so great.
Utoledo is primarily a commuter college. I know maybe one person who lives 'on campus' and even that isn't technically true.

key_points: Commuter-heavy culture makes building a social life harder than typical campuses
One freshman described their first semester as dragging by very slowly
EDM/festival scene preferred over clubs by at least some students
key_quotes: I'm a freshman at the University of Toledo... this semester has just been dragging by a lot for me.
I partied a lot in high school... raves and shows are so much more fun to me [than clubs].
A lot of people go to the bar or just hang out at home.

key_points: Very limited dining data available from student sources
Scott Park campus cafeteria remembered fondly by alumni decades later
No recent student complaints or praise about current dining options found
key_quotes: I loved going to the cafeteria, it was always the best spot. The cooks were funny.
I graduated back in the 80s and lived off campus during my UT years. It was truly memories of a lifetime.

key_points: No student mentions of counseling services or mental health support
ADHD student noted needing multiple paid tools to manage coursework effectively
Commuter culture may isolate students who don't proactively build connections
key_quotes: I'm an ADHD undergraduate Neuroscience & Psychology student... I use a bit of everything: flashcards, [multiple apps] just to maintain my flow state and avoid burnout.
I'm really confused on what I should do... I might be robbed of the college experience by going to a school in a small town.

key_points: Scholarships are a major draw — UT offers competitive merit aid including Presidential Scholarship
International students report receiving significant aid, making UT a budget-friendly option
Scholarship appeal process can yield more money if you ask directly
key_quotes: University of Toledo gave me a 4-year renewable scholarship that is 6,000 a year.
I got a lot of money from University of Toledo and Bowling Green State University.
University of Toledo: $30k ($2500 scholarship) — one of the most affordable options on the list.

key_points: Single-professor courses on Health Science Campus create no-alternative situations if issues arise
Engineering program well-regarded by alumni, especially electrical engineering
Co-requisite advising is inconsistent — overrides handed out freely, causing confusion
key_quotes: Most of the time one subject is taught by one professor. If you get issues with the professor, there is no other choice.
University of Toledo is a great Engineering school. I had great college experience.
I had one professor who almost doesn't show up to class and only sent... [no follow-up resolution described].

key_points: Advisors inconsistently enforce co-requisite rules, creating student confusion
Parking enforcement is aggressive — cop ticketed a DoorDash driver stopped for under a minute
Scholarship communication is slow — applicants wait months with no response
key_quotes: Did anyone get a message back from the Presidential Scholarship? I applied last November but didn't get anything back yet.
The cop still gave me a ticket. I was there with my car running for less than a minute.
Advisors seem more than happy to hand out overrides for these types of situations.

key_points: Toledo described as a small town — students feel limited cultural exposure
Campus is split across multiple locations including a separate Health Science Campus
Surrounding area considered safe enough but not exciting for students seeking city life
key_quotes: I might be robbed of the college experience by going to a school in a small town.
I used to walk and cross over Douglas Ave to go to school every day... University of Toledo is a great school specially for Engineering.
I live off campus in an apartment complex not affiliated with the university. The rent comes out to about $850/month.

key_points: Engineering alumni report strong long-term career success, especially in tech and CS
When compared to Miami University (OH), UT loses on employer name recognition
Bacc2Med program pathway to med school exists but acceptance details are unclear
key_quotes: I got my BS in Electrical Engineering and MS in Computer Science and have been working in the US for 32 years as a Computer Engineer.
I get offered jobs to this day just because I went to Miami. Go to Miami.
I do not recommend the [Bacc2Med] program based on interacting with students in the program.

key_points: Strong scholarship packages make UT one of the most affordable options in Ohio
On-campus housing costs are considered too high relative to off-campus alternatives
International students specifically target UT for low total cost with decent academics
key_quotes: University of Toledo: $30k total cost — lowest on the list for international students.
All for the low price of $3000+ a semester... Lower your prices.
I'm saving a lot of money because I have a decent scholarship.

key_points: Commuter school culture dominates — social life requires active effort to find
Comfortable and homey feel noted, but less prestigious college-town energy
International students, especially Nepali, form a visible and active community
key_quotes: I found Toledo to feel much more comfortable and homey but Miami is a more prestigious school.
Utoledo is primarily a commuter college.
I grew as a person. I wouldn't have done my professional career without [UT].

key_points: **Academics:** One professor per course on Health Science Campus — no recourse if problems arise
**Administration:** Aggressive parking enforcement targets even brief stops in construction zones
**Reputation:** Multiple Ohio natives consistently recommend Miami University over Toledo for both academics and career outcomes
key_quotes: I have seen corruption. I had one professor who almost doesn't show up to class.
I have degrees from both. Go to Miami.
No disrespect to Toledo, but the answer to this one is Miami.

key_points: Bacc2Med program offers a direct medical school pathway not widely known externally
Rocket Solution Central office praised as genuinely helpful for financial and tuition questions
Engineering reputation quietly strong — alumni thriving in careers 30+ years later
key_quotes: Contact Rocket Solution Central to have them look into it for you and break things down. They are pretty helpful and they are completely free.
I got my BS in Electrical Engineering and MS in Computer Science and have been working in the US for 32 years.
University of Toledo is a great Engineering school, I had great college experience.
